records.ety: [OE. delit, OF. delit, deleit, fr. delitier, to delight. See Delight, v. t.]

defs: A high degree of gratification of mind; a high- wrought state of pleasurable feeling; lively pleasure; extreme satisfaction; joy.
quotes: Sounds and sweet airs, that give delight and hurt not.
A fool hath no delight in understanding.

defs: That which gives great pleasure or delight.
quotes: Heaven's last, best gift, my ever new delight.

records.ety: [OE. deliten, OF. delitier, deleitier, F. dlecter, fr. L. delectare to entice away, to delight (sc. by attracting or alluring), intens. of delicere to allure, delight; de- + lacere to entice, allure; cf. laqueus a snare. Cf. Delectate, Delicate, Delicious, Dilettante, Elicit, Lace.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Delighted; p. pr. & vb. n. Delighting.]

defs: To give delight to; to affect with great pleasure; to please highly; as, a beautiful landscape delights the eye; harmony delights the ear.
quotes: Delight our souls with talk of knightly deeds.

defs: To have or take great delight or pleasure; to be greatly pleased or rejoiced; -- followed by an infinitive, or by in.
quotes: I delight to do thy will, O my God.
